analytic.fixed_point: g(N)<N for all N>0 (survival<1); only equilibrium N*=0 => monotone decline, NO spurious positive floor, NO mean-reversion
analytic.order_preservation: g increasing for N < 9.8e10 stems/ha => bigger N1 -> bigger N2 across all realistic densities; no order inversion
analytic.density_response: per-step survival exp(-K N^0.2108 T) strictly DECREASING in N => denser stands thin FASTER (correct self-thinning), driven by POSITIVE lnN1 slope
analytic.contrast_topheight: top-height log-ratio map had NEGATIVE lnH1 slope -> contraction to a step-dependent positive fixed point (pathology). Stems map has no such fixed point because the *T inside exp() forces survival<1; positive lnN1 slope only steepens decline.

CODE_MISMATCH_BUG.severity: HIGH - separate from contraction question, but a real production defect
CODE_MISMATCH_BUG.issue: stand_constraint.py::stand_stems_target implements a SOFTPLUS LOG-RATIO form (N2=N1*exp(-softplus(eta))) that does NOT match the fitted cloglog HAZARD bundle (N2=N1*exp(-exp(eta)*T)).
consequences: No multiplication by T: the per-step mortality intensity is applied identically regardless of step length (5yr vs 10yr vs 20yr steps give same survival fraction). Breaks García time-scaling / exposure.
Reads a nonexistent 'ln_years' coefficient from the bundle (bundle folds years into intercept + *T), so it silently contributes 0 and the years signal is dropped entirely.

softplus(eta) != exp(eta): near the fitted operating point eta~ -3.8, softplus(eta)~0.022 vs exp(eta)~0.022 are close, but they diverge for large |eta|; more importantly the missing *T is the dominant error.
Empirically the CODE form retains far more stems (N100=293 vs 99 for N0=400 frozen; ~3x too many survivors over 100yr) because it applies one weak per-step reduction with no exposure scaling.
CODE_MISMATCH_BUG.recommended_fix: Replace stand_stems_target body with the bundle's hazard form: H=exp(eta); return N1*exp(-H*years). Drop the ln_years term and the softplus. This matches 76_fit_stand_stems.R exactly and restores correct time-scaling.
